content: PowerWorld Kiribathgoda operates daily with early-morning and evening peak usage. Members should complete check-in/check-out through QR or assisted trainer flow.
Only members with active or grace-period subscriptions can enter through member check-in. Staff roles (trainer, manager, admin) can enter without subscription validation.

businessRule.rule: Gate access must always validate current subscription status for members; trainers can still check-in members manually if the QR device fails.

content: Beginner fat-loss starter: 3 days/week, 35-45 minutes/session. Include treadmill incline walk 15 min, bodyweight squats 3x12, assisted rows 3x12, light dumbbell press 3x10, plank 3x30s, cooldown 8 min.

Beginner muscle-gain starter: 4 days/week split. Day A: goblet squat, bench press machine, seated row, shoulder press, core. Day B: leg press, lat pulldown, chest fly, Romanian deadlift variation, core. Progressively increase load while keeping 2-3 reps in reserve.
Members can book personal sessions from the app. Trainers should confirm and track session outcomes. If members request help on floor, prioritize safety/form correction before intensity.

faqPairs.q: How should trainers respond when a member presses the assistance button?
faqPairs.a: Respond quickly, assess safety first, correct form, then adjust load and volume if needed.

content: Always prioritize safety and proper form. Stop exercise if sharp pain, dizziness, or chest discomfort occurs. For diagnosis or medical concerns, direct users to licensed healthcare professionals.

For Kiribathgoda, monthly revenue is mainly driven by renewals of monthly plans and upsell of PT sessions. Managers should watch upcoming expiries, freeze requests, and idle members who have not visited in 14+ days.

businessRule.rule: When churn risk is high (many members near expiry with low visits), focus campaigns on reactivation and short-term discounts instead of only acquiring new members.

content: Use hourly visit peaks to align trainer shifts and equipment maintenance windows. Aim to keep peak-hour occupancy comfortable by opening more floor support and deferring non-critical maintenance.

businessRule.rule: If evening peak occupancy stays above target for several days, temporarily increase trainer coverage or adjust PT sessions away from peak times.

content: Beginner strength foundation should focus on 5 primary movement patterns: squat, hinge, push, pull, and carry. Keep effort around RPE 6-7 for first 3 weeks, then gradually progress to RPE 7-8 while maintaining strict form.

Intermediate hypertrophy progression: 10-18 hard sets per muscle group per week, mostly 6-15 rep range, 1-3 reps in reserve. Use upper/lower or push-pull-legs split depending on available training days.

Advanced members should use block periodization: accumulation, intensification, and deload. Heavy lifts can include low-rep top sets and controlled back-off sets, but fatigue should be managed with planned recovery weeks.

Mobility and recovery sessions should include 8-12 minutes of dynamic warmup, targeted mobility drills for tight regions, and low-intensity cooldown breathing. Use at least 1 lighter recovery day each week.

For endurance goals, combine zone-2 steady cardio with 1-2 interval sessions weekly. Increase total weekly cardio minutes gradually by about 10% and avoid stacking high-intensity intervals on consecutive days.
Progression rule: only increase load after hitting target reps with good form in all prescribed sets. Typical increment is 2.5-5% for upper body and 5-10% for lower body movements.
Regression rule: if form breaks, pain increases, or reps drop sharply, reduce load, volume, or exercise complexity. Technique quality and safety are always prioritized over weight lifted.

For members with limited time, use 30-35 minute sessions with supersets on non-competing muscle groups, short rest periods, and clear priority lifts first. Consistency across week matters more than perfect session duration.
When equipment is unavailable, swap to movement-equivalent alternatives: squat to leg press, barbell row to cable row, bench press to dumbbell press. Keep similar muscle target and effort level.

AI guidance should provide general training advice only. For persistent pain, injury rehabilitation, cardiovascular symptoms, or medication-related concerns, members must consult qualified healthcare professionals.

Manager AI responses should classify trends as improving, flat, or declining using explicit comparison windows (for example, last 7 days vs previous 7 days) and include confidence qualifiers when data is sparse.

When anomalies appear (sudden drop in attendance, spike in incidents, unusual payment decline), recommend quick triage steps: verify data integrity, inspect staffing and equipment status, and trigger targeted member follow-up.

Retention actions should prioritize members with low visit frequency near subscription expiry. Recommend outreach bundles: trainer check-in, short-term challenge plan, and renewal incentive with measurable follow-up.

businessRule.rule: Retention-led campaigns usually improve predictable revenue more than broad discounting.

content: Use attendance heatmaps to align trainer shifts to peak windows and schedule non-urgent tasks in low-traffic periods. This reduces member wait times and improves floor supervision.
If open high-severity equipment incidents persist, prioritize isolation and maintenance before allowing continued use. Communicate temporary alternatives to trainers so sessions remain productive.

For consistency, members should fix 3-4 non-negotiable weekly training slots and prepare simple fallback sessions for busy days. Missing one session is recoverable; missing repeatedly requires schedule redesign.
Workout results improve with basic nutrition habits: adequate protein spread across meals, hydration during training, and consistent sleep. AI should keep recommendations practical and non-medical.
Short-horizon forecasting should use recent rolling averages and known branch patterns (weekday peaks, seasonal behavior) and should be framed as directional guidance, not guaranteed outcomes.
